title: Studies on the Extractive Behaviour of 12-Heteropoly Acids. Part II : Extraction of 12-Molybdophosphoric, 12-Tungstosilicic and 12-Molybdosilicic Acids
author: V. I. Lakshm.anan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The extractive behaviour of 12-molybdophosphoric (H 3 PMo 12 O 40 ), 12-molybdosilicic (H 4 SiMo 12 O 40 ) and 12-tungstosilicic (H 4 SiW 12 O 40 ) acids into various organic solvents have been studied. The order of extraction of H 3 PMo 12 O 40  into oxygenated organic solvents is very similar to that of H 3 PW 12 O 40 . The oxygenated organic solvents extract H 3 PMo 12 O 40  and H 3 PW 12 O 40  better than H 4 SiM 12 O 40  and H 4 SiW 12 O 40 . Equilibria involved in the extraction process are discussed and the extraction constants have been calculated. Extraction data give support to the view that 12-heteropoly acids are extracted into oxygenated organic solvents in the form of solvated ion pairs. From 0·5.  M  LiCl solution, salts of the type LiH 2 M 12 O 40  and LiH 3 M 12 O 40  are extracted into the organic phase (where M = Mo or W).
steps_raw: The extractive behaviour of 12-molybdophosphoric (H 3 PMo 12 O 40 ), 12-molybdosilicic (H 4 SiMo 12 O 40 ) and 12-tungstosilicic (H 4 SiW 12 O 40 ) acids into various organic solvents have been studied. Equilibria involved in the extraction process are discussed and the extraction constants have been calculated. Extraction data give support to the view that 12-heteropoly acids are extracted into oxygenated organic solvents in the form of solvated ion pairs. From 0·5.  M  LiCl solution, salts of the type LiH 2 M 12 O 40  and LiH 3 M 12 O 40  are extracted into the organic phase (where M = Mo or W).
